seoTitle: Fawkner vs Ferguson — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Fawkner and Ferguson. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fawkner and Ferguson.
heading: Fawkner vs Ferguson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fawkner, Victoria and Ferguson, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fawkner has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Ferguson (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fawkner or Ferguson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fawkner has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Ferguson scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fawkner scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Ferguson scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fawkner or Ferguson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fawkner has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
